Andrew Tieu

Bachelor of Science, Mechanical Engineering (Graduating May 2026)

California State Polytechnic University, Pomona

Hello! My name is Andrew Tieu. I am a mechanical engineering student based in Orange County, California, with hands-on experience in CAD modeling, structural analysis, tooling and fixture design, and manufacturing which I have applied to real production environments. This portfolio serves as a supplement to my resume, showcasing the projects and professional experiences I developed throughout my undergraduate studies at Cal Poly Pomona. I actively sought out work that challenged me at different technical levels and produced real, measurable impact. I'm always looking for the next problem worth solving.

Current Projects
Internship:
FXC Corporation / Guardian Parachute

BANSHEE eVTOL UAV
FXC Corporation / Guardian Parachute
Mechanical Engineering Intern

At FXC Corporation in Costa Mesa, CA, I work on parachute and cargo release systems for aerospace and defense applications. The work spans fixture design, manufacturing quality, and technical documentation.

Key contributions:

  • Designed an axial deformation test fixture in SOLIDWORKS — guided press architecture ensuring repeatable alignment and controlled load paths, reducing scrap from operator variability
  • Developed 120+ part drawings and 10+ large assemblies using GD&T per ASME Y14.5M standard
  • Operated FDM and Resin 3D printers for prototype assembly, mechanism simulation, and verification testing
  • Investigated functional nonconformances using DMAIC methodology and authored engineering evaluation reports that drove revisions to inspection criteria.
  • Developed textile pattern drawings for parachute soft goods in AutoCAD under manufacturing engineering, released to production for new product build.
  • Created and managed BOMs tracking over $750,000 worth of assemblies using ERP systems
Cam Retention Fixture and Tooling Rework

Redesigned a cam retention fixture and tooling rework for axial deformation testing, integrating SOLIDWORKS modeling with hands-on manufacturing to improve repeatability and reduce scrap.

  • Designed and prototyped an axial deformation test fixture in SOLIDWORKS, translating deformation requirements into a guided press architecture that ensured repeatable alignment and controlled load paths.
  • Led tooling and fixture rework to standardize operator interaction during deformation testing, improving usability while maintaining consistent axial loading conditions.
  • Implemented a validated deformation fixture that reduced scrap rate by minimizing operator variability and improving repeatability across press-based deformation trials.
Extraction Parachute Jettison System
Modelling and Simulation Under 75,000 lb Load

Modeled the EPJS (Emergency Parachute Jettison System) assembly to validate structural integrity of end plates under increased rated load requirements, integrating SOLIDWORKS assembly modeling with finite element analysis.

  • Modeled the full EPJS actuator assembly in SOLIDWORKS, consisting of end plate, cylindrical barrel, hex fasteners with O-ring face seals, and hydraulic hose barb fitting.
  • Ran Von Mises FEA simulation at 75,000 lb total load (37,500 lb per plate), identifying peak stress concentrations (~1.8 × 10⁵ psi) localized to mounting hole interfaces at the lower bearing edge.
  • Confirmed design margins against material yield limits for flight qualification load cases.
Lean Six Sigma Application:
Dimensional Nonconformance Investigation

Applied the DMAIC framework to identify vendor-induced dimensional nonconformance that was causing activation device failures in a production lot.

Measured 50+ capsules via optical projector to characterize the dimensional distribution. Statistical analysis identified the root cause as a vendor process shift. Implemented corrective rework and worked with the vendor on process controls to prevent recurrence.

  • Collected dimensional data across 50+ parts using optical projection measurement
  • Built frequency distribution charts identifying out-of-spec population
  • Isolated vendor-side process variable as root cause via DMAIC
  • Implemented corrective rework and documented in Engineering Evaluation Report
BANSHEE eVTOL Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV)
Structures & Design

The Battery As iNtegrated Structure High Endurance Experimental Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (BANSHEE UAV) is a multidisciplinary senior design project developing an endurance-optimized electric vertical takeoff and landing platform. I serve on the Structures & Design subteam, contributing to airframe design, structural analysis, and CAD development across the full vehicle architecture

Key contributions:

  • Designed a wing–fuselage disconnection mechanism enabling rapid battery swapping and modular field assembly
  • Collaborated with the aerodynamics and manufacturing subteams to design fairings using DFM principles, increasing the lift-to-drag of aircraft ratio by 42%
  • Defined assembly constraint conventions and CAD modeling techniques to ensure design intent was maintained across all subteam members.
  • Designed a manufacturable landing pad engineered for durability and repeatability across flight test campaigns
  • Performed wing loading and structural stress calculations, validated through SOLIDWORKS CAD simulations
